methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $62M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Amca is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Jai Malik and Eli Giovanetti that has raised $76.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private robotics firms at Series A/B stage and 1 public comparable, representing the frontier-tech robotics sector.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $62M total raised as a baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $125M. Peer-median analysis examines 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—including 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Foundation Robotics ($3.0B, Series A), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), and Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B)—establishing a median valuation of $1.6B with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $106M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $888M. This wide spread reflects material uncertainty inherent in early-stage robotics valuations, where comparable companies demonstrate significant dispersion based on technology differentiation, capital efficiency, and path to deployment scale. The peer-median method dominates the upper bound, while stage-progression anchors the lower bound. Key sensitivities include the pace of customer adoption and capital requirements to reach Series B milestones.
